track_pick.13: Push The Feeling On (MK's Nocturnal Dub)

video_note.13: featured cut = MK's Nocturnal Dub — the chopped-vocal, sub-bass dub UK speed garage was built on
video_note.16: featured cut = the full Original Mix, not the radio edit
video_note.18: featured cut = Kelly G's Bump-N-Go Mix (1997) — the speed-garage remix that turned this 1995 US R&B single into the UKG crossover anthem; the master links the '95 original
video_note.24: playlist plays the full vocal mix (feat. Lain), not the master's radio edit
video_note.25: featured cut = the Sunship Mix — the 2-step remix that made *Flowers* a UKG classic (the master's default is the C&J pop mix)

row_note.5: Larry Levan mixed the whole 1983 Sleeping Bag 12″ — a Paradise Garage cornerstone.
row_note.11: Lead cut of Kerri Chandler's 1993 *Atmosphere E.P.* (Shelter) — New Jersey's deep-garage lineage carried into the '90s.
row_note.14: New Jersey's Todd Edwards, whose cut-up 'sample garage' is the DNA of UK 2-step — the hinge of this whole story.
row_note.15: Armand Van Helden's *Dark Garage Mix* of Sneaker Pimps — widely credited with igniting UK speed garage.
row_note.23: UK garage's mainstream breakthrough — UK #2, Christmas 1999.
